name: manim-dsa

metadata.name: manim-dsa
metadata.summary: A Manim plugin to animate common data structures.

documentation:
A Manim plugin designed to animate common data structures and algorithms

**Manim DSA**, short for _Manim Data Structures & Algorithms_, is a [Manim](https://www.manim.community/) plugin designed to animate common data structures and algorithms. Whether you are an educator looking to enhance your lectures, a student seeking to better understand these concepts, or a content creator making educational videos, Manim DSA provides a robust toolkit to bring abstract concepts to life. ## Table of Contents: - [Installation](#installation) - [Importing](#importing) - [Usage](#usage) - [Documentation](#documentation) - [Help with Manim DSA](#help-with-manim-dsa) - [Contributing](#contributing) - [License](#license) ## Installation To install Manim DSA, you can use `pip`: ```bash pip install manim-dsa ``` If you don't have Manim installed, please refer to the [official Manim documentation](https://docs.manim.community/en/stable/installation.html) for installation instructions. ## Importing Simply use the following line of code to import the package: ```py from manim_dsa import * ``` ## Usage The following is an example `Scene` where an array, a stack and a graph are created. ```python from manim import * from manim_dsa import * class Example(Scene): def construct(self): graph = { 'A': [('C', 11), ('D', 7)], 'B': [('A', 5), ('C', 3)], 'C': [('A', 11), ('B', 3)], 'D': [('A', 7), ('C', 4)], } nodes_and_positions = { 'A': LEFT * 1.5, 'B': UP * 2, 'C': RIGHT * 1.5, 'D': DOWN * 2, } mArray = ( MArray([1, 2, 3], style=MArrayStyle.BLUE) .add_indexes() .scale(0.9) .add_label(Text("Array", font="Cascadia Code")) .to_edge(LEFT, 1) ) mStack = ( MStack([3, 7, 98, 1], style=MStackStyle.GREEN) .scale(0.8) .add_label(Text("Stack", font="Cascadia Code")) .move_to(ORIGIN) ) mGraph = ( MGraph(graph, nodes_and_positions, MGraphStyle.PURPLE) .add_label(Text("Graph", font="Cascadia Code")) .to_edge(RIGHT, 1) ) self.play(Create(mArray)) self.play(Create(mStack)) self.play(Create(mGraph)) self.wait() ``` The result is as follows: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/05307dc9-65d9-4262-ae32-3fb53068c8e4 ## Documentation Documentation is in progress at [ReadtheDocs](https://manim-dsa.readthedocs.io). ## Help with Manim DSA If you need help installing or using Manim DSA, or you need to submit a bug report or feature request, please open an issue. ## Contributing Contributions are welcome! Whether it’s reporting a bug, suggesting new features, or submitting pull requests, any help is greatly appreciated. ## How to Cite Manim DSA To demonstrate the value of Manim DSA, we ask that you cite Manim DSA in your work. Currently, the best way to cite Manim DSA is to go to the [repository page](https://github.com/F4bbi/manim-dsa) (if you aren't already) and click the "cite this repository" button on the right sidebar. This will generate a citation in your preferred format, and will also integrate well with citation managers. For guidance on how to properly cite Manim, please refer to the [Manim GitHub page](https://github.com/ManimCommunity/manim/blob/main/README.md#how-to-cite-manim). ## License Manim DSA is licensed under the MIT License. You are free to use, modify, and distribute this software as long as you include the original license. ## Acknowledgements - **[Manim Community](https://www.manim.community/)**: For creating and maintaining the amazing Manim library. - **[drageelr](https://github.com/drageelr/manim-data-structures)**: For the inspiration behind the array implementation. - **[Verdiana Pasqualini](https://verdianapasqualini.github.io/ManimGraphLibrary)**: For the inspiration behind the graph implementation.

classes.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_stack.MStack

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_stack.MStack.get_spawn_point
documentation:
Calculates the drop point for new elements in the stack.

Returns
-------
Point3D
    The spawn point position in 3D space.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_stack.MStack.append
documentation:
Appends a new value to the top of the stack.

# Parameters

value

: The value to be added to the stack. It will be converted to a string representation.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`MStack` with the newly appended element.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_stack.MStack._append_animation
documentation:
Creates an animation for appending a new value to the stack.

Parameters
----------
value : Any
    The value to be added to the stack. It will be converted to a string representation.
anim_args : dict, optional
    Additional animation arguments.

Returns
-------
Succession
    The animation object representing the append operation.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_stack.MStack.pop
documentation:
Removes the top element from the stack.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`MStack` with the top element removed.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_stack.MStack._pop_animation
documentation:
Creates an animation for removing the top element from the stack.

Parameters
----------
anim_args : dict, optional
    Additional animation arguments.

Returns
-------
Succession
    The animation object representing the pop operation.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_stack.MStack.add_label
documentation:
Adds a label to the stack.

# Parameters

text

: The label text.

direction

: The direction in which to position the label. Default is UP.

buff

: The distance (buffer) between the stack and the label. Default is 0.5.

kwargs :

: Additional keyword arguments that are passed to the function next_to() of the
  underlying add_label method.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`MStack` with the label added.

Manim Stack: a class for visualizing the stack structure using the Manim animation engine.

Parameters
----------
arr : list, optional
    The initial list of values to populate the stack. Default is an empty list.
buff : float, optional
    The buffer (margin) between elements in the stack. Default is 0.1.
style : MStackStyle._DefaultStyle, optional
    The style configuration for the stack elements. Default is MStackStyle.DEFAULT.

classes.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_array.MIndexedElement

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_array.MIndexedElement.set_index
documentation:
Updates the index of the element to a new value.

# Parameters

new_index

: The new index value to set. It will be converted to a string representation.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`MIndexedElement` with the updated index.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_array.MIndexedElement.add_index
documentation:
Adds an index to the element, positioning it relative to the element.

# Parameters

index

: The text object representing the index.

direction

: The direction in which to position the index relative to the element. Default is UP.

buff

: The distance (buffer) between the element and the index.
  Default is DEFAULT_MOBJECT_TO_MOBJECT_BUFFER.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`MIndexedElement` with the updated index.

An extension of the MElement class that includes an index for each element.

Parameters
----------
square : Rectangle
    The rectangle representing the visual boundary of the element.
value : Text
    The text representing the value contained in the element.

classes.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_array.MArray

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_array.MArray.append
documentation:
Appends a new element to the end of the array. If indexing is enabled,
the new element will also be assigned an index based on its position in the array.

# Parameters

value

: The value to append. It will be converted to a string representation.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`MArray` with the newly appended element.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_array.MArray._append_animation
documentation:
Animates the addition of a new element to the array.

Parameters
----------
value : Any
    The value to append. It will be converted to a string representation.
anim_args : dict, optional
    Additional arguments for the animation. Default is None.

Returns
-------
Animation
    An animation that displays the new element being written into the array.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_array.MArray.pop
documentation:
Removes the element at the specified index and shifts all subsequent elements accordingly.
If indexing is enabled, it also updates the indices of the remaining elements.

# Parameters

index

: The index of the element to be removed. Default is -1, which removes the last element.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`MArray` with the specified element removed.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_array.MArray._pop_animation
documentation:
Animates the removal of an element from the array.

Parameters
----------
index : int, optional
    The index of the element to be removed. Default is -1, which removes the last element.
anim_args : dict, optional
    Additional arguments for the animation. Default is None.

Returns
-------
Animation
    An animation showing the element being removed and the indices being updated.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_array.MArray.add_indexes
documentation:
Adds indexes to each element in the array, displaying them in the specified direction.

# Parameters

direction

: The direction in which to display the indices relative to the elements.
  Default is UP.

buff

: The buffer distance between the element and its index.
  Default is DEFAULT_MOBJECT_TO_MOBJECT_BUFFER.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`MArray` with the specified element removed.

# Raises

Exception

: If the specified direction is parallel to the array's growth direction.

# Notes

If indices are already enabled, this method returns immediately without making any changes.

Manim Array: a class for visualizing the array structure using the Manim animation engine.

Parameters
----------
arr : list, optional
    The initial list of values to populate the array. Default is an empty list.
direction : Vector3D, optional
    The direction in which to arrange the elements. Default is RIGHT.
margin : float, optional
    The margin between elements in the array. Default is 0.
style : MArrayStyle._DefaultStyle, optional
    The style configuration for the elements. Default is MArrayStyle.DEFAULT.

functions.name: manim_dsa.utils.utils.set_text
documentation:
Replace the content of an existing Manim Text object with new text while preserving its style.

Parameters
----------
old_manim_text : Text
    The original Manim Text object whose content is being replaced.
new_text : str
    The new text content to set.

Returns
-------
Text
    A new Text object with the updated content, matching the style and position of the original text.

functions.name: manim_dsa.utils.utils.TextReplace
documentation:
Replace one text object with another in a scene using animations.

Parameters
----------
scene : Scene
    The Manim Scene where the animation takes place.
scene_mobj1 : Group
    The group containing `mObj1`, which is replaced in this group.
mObj1 : Text
    The original text object to be replaced.
mObj2 : Text
    The text object whose content will replace `mObj1`.

Notes
-----
The animation consists of fading out the old text (`mObj1`) while animating
the new text (`mObj2`) into its position.

classes.name: manim_dsa.utils.utils.Labelable

documentation: Initialize the Labelable object with no label.

methods.name: manim_dsa.utils.utils.Labelable.add_label
documentation:
Add a label to the object.

Parameters
----------
text : Text
    The Text object to use as the label.
direction : Vector3D, optional
    The direction to place the label relative to the object (default is `UP`).
buff : float, optional
    The distance between the object and the label (default is 0.5).
**kwargs : dict
    Additional keyword arguments for positioning.

Returns
-------
Labelable
    The instance with the label added.

methods.name: manim_dsa.utils.utils.Labelable.has_label
documentation:
Check if the object has a label.

Returns
-------
bool
    `True` if the object has a label, otherwise `False`.

A mixin class that provides functionality to add a label to Manim objects.

Attributes
----------
label : Text or None
    The label associated with the object, if any.

Highlight the object with the specified stroke color and width.

Set the highlight properties.

documentation:
Animation for unhighlighting the object.

Animation

A mixin class that provides functionality to highlight and unhighlight Manim objects.

Attributes
----------
highlighting : VMobject or None

classes.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_collection.MElement

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_collection.MElement.set_value
documentation:
Updates the value of the element.

This method is necessary because the `set_text` method of the `Text` class in Manim
does not function as expected. Instead, this method manually removes the current text
object and replaces it with a new one, re-centering it within the square.

# Parameters

value

: The value to append. It will be converted to a string representation.

# Returns

self

: The updated instance of {class}`MElement` with the new value.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_collection.MElement._set_value_animation
documentation:
Creates an animation for updating the value of the element.

Parameters
----------
value : Any
    The value to append. It will be converted to a string representation.
anim_args : dict, optional
    Additional arguments for the animation. Default is None.

Returns
-------
Animation
    An animation indicating the value change.

Represents an element in a visual collection, consisting of a square and a value.

Parameters
----------
square : Rectangle
    The square that visually represents the element.
value : Text
    The text that displays the value of the element.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_collection.MCollection.pop
documentation:
Removes the element at the specified index and shifts all subsequent elements accordingly.

# Parameters

index

: The index of the element to be removed. Default is -1, which removes the last element.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`MCollection` with the specified element removed.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_collection.MCollection._pop_animation
documentation:
Animates the removal of an element from the collection.

Parameters
----------
index : int, optional
    The index of the element to be removed. Default is -1, which removes the last element.
anim_args : dict, optional
    Additional arguments for the animation. Default is None.

Returns
-------
Animation
    An animation that shows the element being faded out and the remaining elements being shifted.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_collection.MCollection.swap
documentation:
Swaps the positions of two elements in the collection.

# Parameters

i

: The index of the first element to be swapped.

j

: The index of the second element to be swapped.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`MCollection` with the swapped elements.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_collection.MCollection._swap_animation
documentation:
Animates the swap of two elements in the collection.

Parameters
----------
i : int
    The index of the first element to be swapped.
j : int
    The index of the second element to be swapped.
path_arc : float, optional
    The arc angle for the path of the swap animation. Default is PI/2.
anim_args : dict, optional
    Additional arguments for the animation. Default is None.

Returns
-------
Animation
    An animation that shows the elements being swapped.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_collection.m_collection.MCollection.add_label
documentation:
Adds a label to the collection, positioned relative to its elements.

# Parameters

text

: The text label to be added.

direction

: The direction in which to position the label (Default is UP).

buff

: The buffer distance between the label and the element (Default is 0.5).

kwargs :

: Additional keyword arguments that are passed to the function next_to() of the
  underlying add_label method.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`MCollection` with the swapped elements.

An abstract base class representing a collection of MElements.

Parameters
----------
arr : list, optional
    The initial list of values to populate the collection. Default is an empty list.
direction : Vector3D, optional
    The direction in which to arrange the elements. Default is RIGHT.
margin : float, optional
    The distance between elements in the collection. Default is 0.
style : CollectionStyle._DefaultStyle, optional
    The style configuration for the elements. Default is CollectionStyle.DEFAULT.

classes.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.add_node
documentation:
Adds a new node to the graph with a specified name and position.

# Parameters

name

: The name of the node to be added.

position

: The 3D position where the node will be placed. Defaults to ORIGIN.

# Returns

self

: The updated instance of the :class:MGraph' with the new node added.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph._add_node_animation
documentation:
Animates the addition of a new node to the graph.

Parameters
----------
name : str
    The name of the node to be added.
position : Point3D, optional
    The 3D position where the node will be placed. Defaults to ORIGIN.
anim_args : dict, optional
    Additional keyword arguments to be passed to the animation. Defaults to None.

Returns
-------
Animation
    The animation for adding the node.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.add_edge
documentation:
Adds a new edge between two nodes in the graph.

Parameters
----------
node1_name : str
    The name of the first node.
node2_name : str
    The name of the second node.
weight : float, optional
    The weight of the edge. If not provided, the edge will be unweighted.
label_distance : float, optional
    The distance from the edge where the label should be placed. Defaults to 0.2.

Returns
-------
self
    The updated instance of the :class:'MGraph' with the new edge added.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph._add_edge_animation
documentation:
Animates the addition of an edge between two nodes in the graph.

Parameters
----------
node1_name : str
    The name of the first node.
node2_name : str
    The name of the second node.
weight : float, optional
    The weight of the edge. If not provided, the edge will be unweighted.
label_distance : float, optional
    The distance from the edge where the label should be placed. Defaults to 0.3.
anim_args : dict, optional
    Additional arguments for the animation. Default is None.

Returns
-------
Animation
    The animation for adding the edge.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.add_curved_edge
documentation: TODO

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.show_backward_edge
documentation: TODO

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.node_layout
documentation:
Applies a specified layout algorithm to arrange the nodes in the graph.

Parameters
----------
layout : str, optional
    The name of the layout algorithm to be applied to the nodes.
    Defaults to 'kamada_kawai_layout'. Other common layout options may include 'spring_layout', 'circular_layout',
    'shell_layout', and others supported by the underlying graph library.
    A full list of available layouts can be found in the NetworkX documentation:
    https://networkx.org/documentation/stable/reference/drawing.html#module-networkx.drawing.layout

Returns
-------
self
    The updated instance of the :class:'MGraph' with nodes arranged according to the specified layout.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.set_nodes_highlight
documentation:
This method iterates through all the nodes in the graph and applies the specified highlight color and stroke width.

Parameters
----------
color : ManimColor, optional
    The color to be used for highlighting the nodes.
    Defaults to RED.
width : float, optional
    The stroke width of the highlight.
    Defaults to 8.

Returns
-------
self
    The updated instance of the MGraph with all nodes highlighted.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.set_edges_highlight
documentation:
This method iterates through all the edges in the graph and applies the specified highlight color and stroke width.

Parameters
----------
color : ManimColor, optional
    The color to be used for highlighting the edges.
    Defaults to RED.
width : float, optional
    The stroke width of the highlight.
    Defaults to 8.

Returns
-------
self
    The updated instance of the :class:'MGraph' with all edges highlighted.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.add_label
documentation:
Adds a label to the graph with specified alignment and buffer.

Parameters
----------
text : Text
    The label text to be added to the graph.
direction : Vector3D, optional
    The direction in which the label should be positioned relative to the graph.
    Defaults to UP.
buff : float, optional
    The distance between the graph and the label.
    Defaults to 0.5.
**kwargs
    Additional keyword arguments that are passed to the function next_to() of the
    underlying add_label method.

Returns
-------
self
    The updated instance of the :class:'MGraph' with the label added.

A class that represents a node (or vertex) of the graph.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.Edge.weighted

Assigns a label to the edge, indicating that it is weighted.

label

self

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.Edge.is_weighted
documentation:
Checks if the edge is weighted by examining the presence of a label.

bool

documentation:
Highlights the edge with a specified color and stroke width.

stroke_width

self

stroke_color : ManimColor, optional

stroke_width : float, optional
    The width of the stroke used for highlighting. Defaults to 8.
anim_args : dict, optional
    Additional arguments to be passed to the animation creation. Defaults to None.

Returns
-------
Animation

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.Edge.set_highlight
documentation:
Sets the highlight properties for the edge.

# Parameters

stroke_color

: The color to be used for highlighting the edge. Defaults to RED.

stroke_width

: The width of the stroke used for highlighting. Defaults to 8.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the {class}`Edge` with the applied highlight.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.Edge._get_line_start_end
documentation:
Abstract method to determine the start and end points of a line between two nodes.

This method should be implemented to calculate the exact positions where the line
should start and end, taking into account the positions and radii of the nodes.

Parameters
----------
node1_center : Point3D
    The 3D coordinates of the center of the first node.
node2_center : Point3D
    The 3D coordinates of the center of the second node.
node1_radius : float
    The radius of the first node.
node2_radius : float
    The radius of the second node.

Returns
-------
Tuple[Point3D, Point3D]
    A tuple containing two `Point3D` objects representing the start and end points
    of the line connecting the two nodes.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.Edge._get_label_position
documentation:
Abstract method to determine the position of the weight relative to the edge.

Parameters
----------
label_distance : float
    The distance from the line or edge where the label should be positioned.

Returns
-------
Point3D
    The `Point3D` coordinates representing the position of the label.

An abstract class that represents an edge in the graph.

Parameters
----------
line : Line or ArcBetweenPoints
    The line or arc that visually represents the edge between two nodes.
arrow : bool, optional
    If True, an arrowhead is added to the edge. Defaults to True.

inner_classes.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.StraightEdge

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.StraightEdge.weighted
documentation:
Assigns a label (the weight) to the edge and positions it relative to the edge.

# Parameters

label

: The label to be assigned to the edge.

label_distance

: The distance from the edge to position the label. Defaults to 0.3.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the :class:Edge' with the assigned label.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.StraightEdge._get_line_start_end
documentation:
Determines the start and end points of the line based on node positions and radii.

Parameters
----------
node1_center : Point3D
    The 3D coordinates of the center of the first node.
node2_center : Point3D
    The 3D coordinates of the center of the second node.
node1_radius : float
    The radius of the first node.
node2_radius : float
    The radius of the second node.

Returns
-------
tuple[Point3D, Point3D]
    A tuple containing two Point3D objects representing the start and end points of the line.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.StraightEdge._get_label_position
documentation:
Calculates the position of the label (the weight) relative to the edge.

Parameters
----------
label_distance : float
    The distance from the edge to position the label.

Returns
-------
Point3D
    The 3D coordinates where the label should be positioned.

Represents a straight edge in the graph, connecting two nodes with a straight line.

Parameters
----------
line : Line
    The Line object representing the edge.
node1_center : Point3D
    The 3D coordinates of the center of the first node.
node2_center : Point3D
    The 3D coordinates of the center of the second node.
node1_radius : float
    The radius of the first node.
node2_radius : float
    The radius of the second node.
arrow : bool, optional
    Whether to include an arrow on the edge. Defaults to True.

inner_classes.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.CurvedEdge

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.CurvedEdge.weighted
documentation:
Assigns a label (the weight) to the edge and positions it relative to the edge.

# Parameters

label

: The label to be assigned to the edge.

label_distance

: The distance from the edge to position the label. Defaults to 0.3.

# Returns

self

: The instance of the :class:Edge' with the assigned label.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.CurvedEdge._get_line_start_end
documentation:
Calculates the start and end points of the arc considering node positions, radii, and the start angle.

This method computes the positions where the edge should start and end relative to the given nodes,
taking into account their radii and the starting angle.

Parameters
----------
node1_center : Point3D
    The 3D coordinates of the center of the first node.
node2_center : Point3D
    The 3D coordinates of the center of the second node.
node1_radius : float
    The radius of the first node.
node2_radius : float
    The radius of the second node.
start_angle : float, optional
    The angle between the edge direction and the line's start direction. Defaults to PI/3.

Returns
-------
tuple[Point3D, Point3D]
    A tuple containing two `Point3D` objects representing the start and end points of the edge.

methods.name: manim_dsa.m_graph.m_graph.MGraph.CurvedEdge._get_label_position
documentation:
Calculates the position of the label relative to the edge.

This method computes where the label should be placed based on the distance from the edge and the arc of the edge.

Parameters
----------
label_distance : float
    The distance from the edge at which the label should be positioned.

Returns
-------
Point3D
    The 3D coordinates where the label should be positioned relative to the edge.

Represents a curved edge in the graph, connecting two nodes with an arc.

Parameters
----------
line : ArcBetweenPoints
    The ArcBetweenPoints object representing the edge.
node1_center : Point3D
    The 3D coordinates of the center of the first node.
node2_center : Point3D
    The 3D coordinates of the center of the second node.
node1_radius : float
    The radius of the first node.
node2_radius : float
    The radius of the second node.
arrow : bool, optional
    Whether to include an arrow on the edge. Defaults to True.
node_angle : float, optional
    The angle between the line connecting the nodes and the direction of the arc. Defaults to PI/3.
arc_angle : float, optional
    The angle of the arc between the two nodes. Defaults to PI/3.

Manim Graph: a class for visualizing the graph structure using the Manim animation engine.

# Parameters

graph

: > list\[list\[tuple[str, str | int]\]\] or dict\[str, list\[tuple[str, str | int]\]\] for weighted graph

  The graph data, which can be weighted or unweighted.

nodes_position

: A dictionary mapping node labels to their positions as 3D vectors. Defaults to an empty dict.

style

: The visual style to be applied to the graph. Defaults to MGraphStyle.DEFAULT.
